{{Quote|A gambler's paradise if ever there was one. Sabacc tables stretch from wall-to-wall, but if that's not your cup of lum, take heart... Nearly every game of chance ever devised is ready and waiting to take your money. With 99 levels and 1,001 different amusements, they say you can't lose at the Ace. But I wouldn't bet on it.|[[Cohden K'Reye]]|<ref name="Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy">''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]''</ref>}}
The '''Ace of Sabres''' was an upscale resort on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kluistar]], located in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]]. It was located in the middle of a dense woodland on a continent also owned by the [[Ace Entertainment Corporation]].<ref name="WHSV p5" />

==Layout and features==
[[File:Security_at_Ace_of_Sabers.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Head of security [[Dunan Par'Ell]] investigates a cheater at the Ace.]]

Known as a [[Gambling/Legends|gambler's]] paradise and a masterpiece of architecture and modern entertainment, the Ace of Sabres consisted of a hotel boasting ninety-nine levels and one thousand and one different amusements that, according to the [[holo-brochure]], would take a standard month to fully experience. It included seven themed restaurants, hundreds of leisure activities facilities, three nightclubs, two dance halls and a dozen separate [[Casino/Legends|casinos]]<ref name="WHSV p5" /> and gambling halls. There was also an exclusive thirteenth casino—[[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]-renowned [[Sabre Club]]—that catered only to VIPs including royalty and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] nobility.<ref name="WHSV p6">''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'' (page 6)</ref>

Hotel rooms ranged in price from an economy stay at twenty [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] a night to a penthouse suite costing one hundred credits. Four-star meals could set a diner back thirty credits, while drinks ranged from anything currently on tap to imports and costly vintages.<ref name="WHSV p6" /> High-ranking Imperials, royals and corporate officers were offered free getaways, under the unspoken agreement that these indebted VIPs would do favors in turn for the Ace Entertainment Corporation.<ref name="WHSV p10">''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'' (page 10)</ref>

The gambling halls offered a variety of card games, including [[Liar's Cut/Legends|Liar's Cut]], [[Double-Down]] and [[Death Star Bluff]], with an entire wing devoted to [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] tables. High-tech gaming machines provided games of electronic chance such as [[StarSlot Machine]]s [[Blinkode]]s, [[Warp-top]], [[Spatz]], the [[Jubilee Wheel (game)|Jubilee Wheel]] and the [[Orbiter]]. Games like [[Holo-Wing]], [[Shockball 3]] and [[Rancor Hunter]] could be played against live or computer-simulated opponents. The tables of the median, comfort-oriented halls were fitted with adjustable [[Sound-dampening field|dampener fields]] to eliminate background noise, and localized, voice-operated lighting could be dimmed or brightened to suit individual players. Smoke-filled areas could be purified in nanoseconds with miniature air processors. Despite these technological advancements, some casinos eschewed creature comforts for more traditional atmosphere, although their clientèle tended towards the rowdy.<ref name="WHSV p6" />

==History==
Boredom was forbidden at the Ace, and the company offered free vacation planning for its guests, as well as opportunity to camp in the surrounding woodland with rental equipment<ref name="WHSV p5" /> including backpacks, maps and personal transport.<ref name="WHSV p6" /> However, some of the more attractive campers were at risk of being sold into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] to the notorious [[Aubro Ahntanda]] as part of a black-market deal by the Ace's shadowy owners.<ref name="WHSV p10" /> At any given time the pots in the gambling halls were filled with credits, jewels, [[Datacard/Legends|data disks]] containing military or scientific information, and even [[holodeed]]s to [[Droid/Legends|droids]], [[Starship/Legends|starships]], corporations and entire planets.<ref name="WHSV p6" /> [[Jhyarra]] was one of the most popular entertainers to perform at the Ace of Sabres.<ref name="WHSV p15">''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'' (page 15)</ref>

During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], [[Gandin T'Noull]] was the manager of the Ace,<ref name="WHSV p7">''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'' (page 7)</ref> although the Ace Entertainment Corporation itself was secretly managed by [[Sibarra]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], who installed [[Holocamera/Legends|holocameras]] and [[Listening device/Legends|audio eavesdropper]]s throughout the resort to steal sensitive information from the rich clientèle from within his fortified control room in the resort's underground levels.<ref name="WHSV p10" /> [[Dunan Par'Ell]] and [[Universal Guardians, Inc.]]<ref name="WHSV p5" /> provided the resort's minimal but top-quality security.<ref name="WHSV p7" /> No droids, [[Datapad/Legends|datapads]] or weapons were allowed in the casinos, nor were personal cards or die permitted. The door featured top-of-the-line contraband detectors, and violators were immediately removed from the premises with no refund.<ref name="WHSV p6" /> Rumors suggested that certain individuals were quietly disposed of by those behind the scenes, perhaps by becoming permanently lost while walking in the woods. Robbery, assassinations and thievery were not uncommon, although said to be carried out only by the Ace's staff upon careless clients.<ref name="WHSV p7" /> The ex–[[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Cha-Val Sha-Vak]] was among those hired to carry out Sibarra's dirty work, and often picked up assignments that Par'Ell could not undertake.<ref name="WHSV p12">''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'' (page 12)</ref>

As of this era, millions visited the Ace of Sabres every year.<ref name="WHSV p11">''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'' (page 11)</ref> The [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] scoundrel [[Cohden K'Reye]] gave the resort a rating of "three supernovas" while compiling his tour guide to the [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]] of the galaxy.<ref name="WHSV p5" /> [[Tovric]] attempted to assassinate [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Kollrin]] after a game of Liar's Cut, but was apprehended by Par'Ell before his hidden blade could touch the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] dignitary.<ref name="WHSV p8">''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'' (page 8)</ref> [[Herglic/Legends|Herglic]] [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] and compulsive gambler [[Johzan B'oh]] could almost always be found at the establishment.<ref name="AA p54">''[[Alien Anthology]]'' (Page 54)</ref> Other notable regulars included [[Karrison Lee]], [[Solimon Dambrizi]], [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Eriann Strathmore]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Ariss Lyjan]] and [[Udo Broxin]].<ref name="WHSV p12" />

The Ace of Sabres was named for a sabacc card.
